name: Mitophagy Enhancers Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which known source-completeness gaps for Mitophagy Enhancers need repair, and does resolving them change the coverage verdict or public boundaries?

summary: Coverage repair staged public records for the high-priority mitophagy gaps identified in the June 10 surveillance assessment. The staged bundle adds PMID 42058113 as renal-calculi safety context, PMID 40839339/NCT04783207 as young-athlete limiting context, NCT06274749, NCT07231783, and NCT05921266 as active or completed no-results urolithin A registry context, and refreshed registry metadata for NCT06556706, ENERGIZE, ATLAS, and MitoImmune. The staged outlook modernizes the Mitophagy Enhancers support map while keeping evidence_stage human_biomarker_signal, momentum steady, and confidence low.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repair changes public source, study, finding, registry, safety, and support-map coverage, but it does not change the stage, momentum, confidence, or core interpretation. Public staging is warranted because the latest coverage assessment stayed thin due to missing safety, active-registry, limiting-context, registry-metadata, and support-map coverage.

search_log.summary: Confirmed current registry status and dates for staged or refreshed urolithin A records. NCT06274749 and NCT07231783 are recruiting with no posted results; NCT05921266 and NCT06556706 are completed with no posted results; NCT04783207 is completed with a PubMed result but no registry results section; ENERGIZE, ATLAS, and MitoImmune remain completed with PubMed result sources represented locally.

Confirmed that PMID 42058113 is a Kidney International Reports research letter/case series identifying urolithin A in urinary stones from eight older adults and that PMID 40839339 is a Sports Medicine RCT of 1000 mg/day urolithin A in 42 highly trained young male distance runners.
Checked the full renal-calculi article text for patient count, ages, stone composition range, diet-versus-supplement exposure, and safety caveats before staging the safety finding.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Staged as recruiting no-results trial-watch context because the older-adult study includes mitochondrial respiration and brain oxidative-metabolism endpoints.
Staged as recruiting no-results trial-watch context because the study directly tests whether Mitopure changes muscle strength in healthy middle-aged adults.
Staged as completed no-results trial-watch context because the study includes vascular and mitochondrial biomarker endpoints in middle-aged adults with obesity.
Staged as result-bearing PubMed context rather than registry-results context; the young-athlete trial bounds performance and aging interpretation.
Refreshed as the key completed no-results frailty watch item.

excluded_sources.title: The Effects of Protein Supplementation With or Without Urolithin A During Single-leg Immobilization
excluded_sources.reason: Active-not-recruiting immobilization and mitochondrial-respiration study with no posted results. It was not staged because the intervention is protein with or without urolithin A and the context is disuse physiology rather than aging or direct mitophagy-enhancer coverage.

excluded_sources.title: Evaluation of Urolithin A and Fisetin on Improving Sleep and Aging Biomarkers
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting no-results urolithin A/fisetin trial with combination-intervention attribution limits. Keep for future surveillance rather than staging as mitophagy-specific support.

excluded_sources.title: Impact of a Novel Brain Longevity Supplement Containing the Postbiotic Urolithin A (Mitopure) on Cognitive Function and Related Health Outcomes
excluded_sources.reason: Active-not-recruiting no-results cognition and brain-longevity supplement study with a multi-ingredient product boundary. It may matter later if results post, but it was not staged as direct mitophagy evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Influence of the Urolithin A on the Population With BMI Higher Than 30 During Restricted Eating
excluded_sources.reason: Enrolling-by-invitation no-results BMI and restricted-eating study with broad metabolic, muscle, inflammatory, vascular, and epigenetic-aging endpoints. It is adjacent registry context, but not prioritized over the staged older-adult, strength, and completed obesity registries.

excluded_sources.title: The Mitopure Challenge to Detect Levels of Urolithin A in Dried Blood Spots
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting methods and exposure-detection registry for dried blood spot urolithin A measurement. Useful for exposure-method context, not efficacy, safety, or aging-stage support.

excluded_sources.title: Rejuvenating Inter-organellar Communication Via Mitophagy in Ageing and Neurodegeneration
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level urolithin A and mitophagy mechanism context. The repair prioritized primary trial, safety, and registry records over another broad review.

excluded_sources.title: Age-related sarcopenia and the gut microbiome: mechanistic insights into the gut-muscle axis and potential microbiome based therapeutic interventions
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level gut-muscle-axis context with urolithin A mention. Useful for taxonomy with microbiome-metabolite restoration but not direct enough to stage under Mitophagy Enhancers.

notes: The staged repair intentionally keeps the public evidence stage at human_biomarker_signal.
PMID 42058113 is included as safety-boundary evidence, not proof that direct urolithin A supplements cause urinary stones.
NCT04783207/PMID 40839339 is included as limiting functional and taxonomy context because it is young-athlete performance/recovery evidence rather than aging evidence.
NCT05814705, NCT06990256, NCT07060898, NCT07377136, and NCT04985630 were explicitly excluded or deferred to avoid overloading the public mitophagy map with adjacent no-results records.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity, interpretation/forecast, safety-limitations, and taxonomy-mapping reviews for the staged repair bundle.
If approved and published, return Mitophagy Enhancers to ordinary surveillance with watch items for frail older-adult results, active urolithin A registries, and safety reports.
